hook: Recovery gets cleaner when you stop negotiating with what already happened and start grieving what it cost you.
body: Grief is slower than analysis and often more useful than endlessly reviewing the evidence.
voices: The School of Life, Brene Brown

hook: Men often stay in forensic mode because problem-solving feels safer than mourning.
body: Some pain needs to be felt and integrated, not solved like a puzzle.
voices: The School of Life, Brene Brown

hook: Some pain needs to be felt and integrated, not solved like a puzzle.
body: You may understand exactly why the relationship ended and still need time to let your body believe it too.
voices: The School of Life, Brene Brown

body: Give grief a place in the process instead of making analysis do all the emotional work.
